title: WildFly mod_cluster Node Health and Drain Events
dataSources: `index=web` `sourcetype=jboss:server` mod_cluster / MCMP status lines from Undertow mod_cluster subsystem
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder; Splunk Add-on for JMX (`Splunk_TA_jmx`); file monitoring for `jboss:server`, structured audit for `wildfly:audit`
spl:
index=web sourcetype="jboss:server"
| regex _raw="(?i)(mod_cluster|MCMP|STATUS.*DISABLED|node.*failed)"
| stats latest(_time) as last_event count by host
| where count > 0
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw logs or metrics.
description: mod_cluster informs Apache httpd which WildFly nodes are healthy. MCMP errors or DISABLED states mean traffic may stick to bad nodes.
value: Keeps HTTP load balanced fairly across the WildFly cluster.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for signs that keeps HTTP load balanced fairly across the WildFly cluster.
implementation: Ensure mod_cluster advertises correctly; compare with HTTP front-end health checks. Alert on DISABLED or failed node chatter.
visualization: Time series for pool/queue metrics, top stats for failures, event timeline for deploys.
knownFalsePositives: Spikes or gaps can follow approved maintenance, load tests, or short collection outages. We add a second signal and a change window before escalating.
